x.name: Kurt Elster
x.bio:
CTO. MBA. The @Shopify expert your developer follows. 
Consultant, host of The Unofficial Shopify Podcast, app dev, & agency owner. Shopify Partner since 2011.

tweets.text:
Bundle pricing works best when it's "may as well" pricing. It's better to raise prices on individual products but drop prices on the bundle. The closer the bundle price is to the hero product's retail price, the more compelling the bundle becomes.
Example: @TacticalBabyGea https://t.co/5vASO2e7Dv
Thanks for hosting a great panel at @shopify NY, @stephan_peralta & co! https://t.co/PFB5oitiIO

tweets.text:
What he said.

I see too many founders optimizing towards top line growth "SCALE BRO!!" instead of maximizing free cash into their pockets.

Over the last 8 years I've made almost as much money from my investments compounding than I did from my first 2 exits.

If the average person can use AI to get their work done in half the time. 

Do they use the rest to do more work?

Or

Do they just take more time for other things?

Outside of entrepreneurs, I think most people choose to take the free time.
